To remove inactive users, we first define a stored procedure in MySQL that handles the deletion and logging process.
The procedure begins by starting a transaction to ensure data integrity throughout the operation.
We then select all user IDs from the users table where the status is marked as Inactive.
These selected IDs are inserted into the status_log table with the current timestamp and their previous status.
Next, the actual deletion occurs from the users table for the identified inactive records.
If any error arises during these steps, the transaction is rolled back to prevent partial data loss.
The error handling block captures the specific exception and returns it to the caller for review.
This approach guarantees that every deletion is recorded before the record is permanently removed from the system.
The status_log table serves as an immutable audit trail for all status changes made by this procedure.
You can call this stored procedure whenever you need to clean up inactive accounts from your database.
The total count of affected rows is returned to inform the administrator of the operation scope.
Error messages are formatted clearly to assist in debugging any potential issues during execution.
Total records processed will be logged for future reference and compliance auditing purposes.
